text: fingers . She wasn’t defined by any single trait, just as he wasn’t defined by any single part. He was no more his optics than she was her eyes, no more his actuators than she was her muscles. She was a sum of her parts, and somehow independent of them. His processors couldn’t quite comprehend the notion — whatever logic he’d been made to operate on couldn’t explain it — but it was the truth . Her eyes enticed him with the spark of life they carried. Her lips demanded his attention each time they fluidly changed with her emotions. The movements of her limbs were a language all their own; abstract, mysterious, and relentlessly compelling. Her humor, though sometimes beyond his grasp, leant an etherealness to her presence. And her willpower, strong as steel, was more admirable than it was frustrating . She existed in this world, had been beaten down by it, but she had never surrendered. Instead, she lifted her chin, displayed her scars, and pushed on. Never allowing the flame of hope inside her to be extinguished. It was her life force; everything that was her . He could not resist . Her eyes fluttered beneath their closed lids. Dreamin g, a distant memory told him . The closest he could manage was through simulations, which rarely included visual or audio components. At heart, it was all numbers — probability and complex calculations based on a variety of data, a cold, mathematical view of what might or might not be. He could take existing components from his memory — images and sounds — and combine them into something different, even alter them slightly, but not make something truly new . Lara’s dances alone were proof that she could create at will . Ronin lightly ran the tip of his finger along her arm, from shoulder to elbow. She stirred, rolling onto her back with a little smile on her lips, but didn’t wake. Was her expression in response to his touch? Did she recognize it, even in sleep ? If he woke her and initiated sex, he doubted she’d resist, despite her exhaustion. His optics trailed down, from her chin to the lines of her neck, over her collarbone and to the gentle slopes of her breasts. If he took her nipple in his mouth and caressed it with his tongue, how would she react ? His focus dipped to the short, red hair between her legs. What if he put his mouth there ? In the years since his reactivation, Ronin had coupled only rarely. There’d been pleasure in it. The fulfillment of desires. And, twice, he’d crossed into the White. Never for more than a second or two, but it had come. The cessation of all processes, of all inputs, save an intense, fleeting explosion of gratification . The way Lara’s body had wrapped around his, had welcomed him inside hungrily, every movement she made — she’d sent waves of electric through him. Lara had brought him to the White, tossed him across the threshold and left him to drift, aware only of the feel of her body and the overwhelming pleasure she gave him . There’d been no room, no bed, no sounds but hers — her moans, her heartbeat, her panting breaths. No Warlord or Cheyenne, no Dust. Only that white space, that blankness, to be filled with her . He’d lost nearly ten seconds when his functions returned to normal . His penis stiffened again. Why not wake her? There was time. Time for her to rest as needed, time for them to make plans. She had given herself to him, and he was lost in her; their coupling left him wanting more . She just lost her sister . Images of Tabitha rose to the forefront. Somehow, he stopped himself from superimposing Lara’s face on her sister’s body. Her life could end in an instant … Lara had given herself to him for comfort. To be distracted from her pain, as long as their bodies were intertwined . He couldn’t bring himself to wake her. Couldn’t be so selfish . Ronin withdrew his hand, settling it atop the blanket between them. His want for her was undiminished, but what they’d done was no sign of deeper emotional connection; she had needed, and he provided. There was no guarantee she’d want him again. No guarantee she wouldn’t regret it when she woke up . He’d been alone for so long that it shouldn’t matter. Ultimately, her feelings toward him were unimportant. Weren’t they? Why was the notion of her rejection so unsettling? Why did he crave more ? He sat up, optics losing focus as his processors turned inward . What if she’d been right? What if bots weren’t truly alive ? The way he felt when he was with her, no matter what they were doing, was that the way humans felt all the time? These emotions couldn’t be the sole criteria for life … So why did his existence prior to Lara seem so muted in comparison ? It’s…it’s about what you experience in that time, you know? About the joy you find … The bed creaked, and the blanket rustled. He swung his optics to Lara. She lay on her side, knees drawn up. One of her arms was extended, her fingers only a few inches from his thigh, as though she were reaching for the spot he’d been in a moment ago . He slid out of the bed and opened the chest just enough to pull out one of the spare blankets inside, careful not to spill any of the tools on the floor. After turning off the light, he laid down beside Lara, spreading the blanket over them, and drew her body against his.
